MATTHEW 18

10 "Take heed that you do not despise one of these little ones, for I
say to you that in heaven their angels always see the face of My
Father who is in heaven.

IN HEAVEN, THE LITTLE ONES’ ANGELS ALWAYS SEE THE FACE
OF MY FATHER

He calls the little ones not those who are really little, but those who are
so esteemed by the multitude, the poor, the objects of contempt, the un-
known ... It is evident that the saints have angels, or even all men.

St. John Chrysostom. Homily LIX on Matthew XVIII, 4, 5. B#54, pp.
367-368.

No man will see God’s face and live; it is the angels of the ‘little ones’ in
the Church, who ‘continually behold the face of the Father in heaven.’ Thus
it is impossible for us now to be capable of the sight of His glorious ap-
pearance ...

St. Basil the Great. On the Psalms, 33.17 (34.16). B#13, p. 91.

Also see Tuesday, 9th Week after Pentecost.

THE ANGELS OF THE LITTLE ONES

The angels described here are those assigned to watch over the children,
and this proves the doctrine of guardian angels.

C. I. Scofield D.D. Scofield Reference Bible, note on Matthew 18:10.
B#999, p. 42.

THE HUMILITY OF THE CHILD

The child is our model of humility and trust.
19th WEEK AFTER PENTECOST (19 APe)
The little ones mentioned here represent all who are meek.

St. Ambrose of Milan. On the Gospel of Luke, 8.55. B#42, p. 290.

LUKE 9

12 When the day began to wear away, the twelve came and said to Him,
Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding towns and
country, and lodge and get provisions ...

THEY ALL ATE AND WERE FILLED

But what was the result of the miracle? It was the satisfying a large
multitude with food ...

St. Cyril of Alexandria. Commentary on St Luke, Homily 48. B#42, p.
215.

28 And it came to pass, about eight days after these sayings, that He took
Peter, John, and James and went up on the mountain to pray.

THE APPEARANCE OF HIS FACE WAS ALTERED, AND HIS ROBE
BECAME WHITE AND GLISTENING. THEN BEHOLD, TWO MEN
TALKED WITH HIM, WHO WERE MOSES AND ELIJAH

He goes up into the mountain taking with Him three chosen disciples; and
is transformed to so surpassing and godlike a brightness ...

St. Cyril of Alexandria. Commentary on the Gospel of St. Luke, Homily
51. B#42, p. 227.

LUKE 18

1 Then He spoke a parable to them, that men always ought to pray and not
lose heart, saying:

ON THE PERSISTENT WIDOW AND ON PRAYER

The person praying must stretch out holy hands ...

Origen. On Prayer, IX.1-X.2. B#7A, pp. 98-101.

10 "Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other
a tax collector.

THE PUBLICAN AND THE PHARISEE

Many are the faults of the Pharisee ...

St. Cyril of Alexandria. Commentary on the Gospel of St. Luke, Homily
121. B#42, p. 484.

JOHN 14

1 "Let not your heart be troubled; you believe in God, believe also in Me.

I AM THE WAY, AND THE TRUTH, AND THE LIFE

In his quest for the truth, man has attempted to solve the problem of truth
... Only in the wondrous person of the God-man Christ has all the eternal
Truth without residue been revealed.

Archimandrite Justin Popovic. The Supreme Value and Infallible Crite-
rion, B#80A, Vol. 4, pp. 115-116, 119-120, 136, 138.

SIXTH WEEK OF PASCHA

SATURDAY Acts 20:7-12; John 14:10-21

ACTS 20

7 Now on the first day of the week, when the disciples came together to
break bread ...

JOHN 14

10 "Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in Me?

I WILL PRAY THE FATHER, AND HE WILL GIVE YOU ANOTHER
HELPER, THAT HE MAY ABIDE WITH YOU FOREVER, EVEN THE
SPIRIT OF TRUTH

We have so much confidence in the deity of the Spirit whom we adore,
that we will begin our teaching concerning His Godhead by fitting to Him
the names which belong to the Trinity ...

St. Gregory of Nazianzus. Fifth Theological Oration, 3, B#7, p. 195.

THE STATUS OF CHRIST

Christ Himself gives evidence of the nature of our life in Him through the
sacrament of the flesh and blood imparted to us, when He says ... 'Since I
live, you also will live ... and I am in you' (John 14:19) ...

St. Hilary of Poitiers. Concerning the Trinity, 8.15. V. The Church and
the Eucharist. B#15, p. 57.
